differentiators: Coordination, constructability thinking, early cost clarity
New market entry — hungry for relationships and wins

strongFit: Mixed-use urban development (Edgewater Public Market, Littleton Mixed Use)
Hospitality — hotels, boutique restaurants, adaptive reuse (Courtyard Mile High, Catbird Hotel, AC Hotel RiNo, Warwick Hotel)
Institutional (education, healthcare, government — Founders Park, Harvard Square Memory Care)
Design-forward / boutique projects (Catbird Hotel, Jacques French, God Save the Cream)
Complex repositioning / additions / renovations (SW Denver YMCA, Warwick Hotel)
possibleFit: Senior living / memory care (proven: Harvard Square Memory Care)
concreteScopeOnly: ANY commercial project where a competitor GC is building
Structural concrete scope on projects Bristlecone didn't win as GC
Action: call the winning GC, not the developer
notAFit: Small projects under ~$2M (GC scope) or under ~$1M (concrete scope)
projectFit.bestClients: Private developers with repeat programs — large nationals or sharp boutique groups open to early collaboration. Especially where a project needs help getting 'unstuck' from cost or feasibility.

scoringGuidance.actionability: Can Tom take a specific action? Parse his network for intros, make a phone call, send a LinkedIn message, submit quals? Score 9-10. 'The market is growing' with no specific action = score 3.
scoringGuidance.timing: Sites going under contract or pre-planning = 10. Just entitled = 9. Permitted and bidding = 8. Under construction = 5 (concrete scope still viable, GC can still change). Completed = 1. Tom says: years before a project starts is fine.
scoringGuidance.projectFit: Design-forward ground-up or complex adaptive reuse = 10. Distressed/foreclosed project needing rescue = 9. Small TI or single-family = 1. Concrete-scope-only on a large project = 7.
scoringGuidance.projectScale: Projects $10M+ for GC scope score highest. $2-10M solid. Under $2M filter out for GC. Concrete scope: $1M+ threshold.
scoringGuidance.competitivePosition: Project with no GC named = 9. Project with GC but Tom knows someone on the team = 7. Known developer's project = boost +2. Known architect's project = boost +2. Competitor GC won the job = 6 (concrete scope lead).
scoringGuidance.gcFieldNote: GC field blank is a SIGNAL worth noting but does NOT dominate scoring. Tom: 'I don't care if there's a GC filled out or not.' GC assigned = concrete scope opportunity.
scoringGuidance.pursuitMatch: Tom's process: network intro first, then phone, LinkedIn, email, text, quals package. Action items should match this flow — e.g., 'Check your network for intros to Fields Development' before 'Send quals to owner.'

clientAdvocates: Tom has referral program active — names in his network. Tag referral leads with source.

emailSchedule.urgentAlerts: same-day for items scoring 9+ with time-sensitive deadlines (optional)

additionalRecipients: Phase 2 — CEO gets KPI view, precon gets bid dates

kpiMetrics.phase1: Raw leads surfaced → qualified leads → pipeline stages (weekly in email)
kpiMetrics.phase2: Full dashboard with estimated project start dates for cash flow forecasting
kpiMetrics.ceoNeeds: Data-oriented: lead volume, pipeline value, stage progression, win rate

fewShotExamples.title: Halo Vista Mixed-Use — N Phoenix (Empire Group)
fewShotExamples.reasoning: Empire Group is a target developer. Mixed-use is Bristlecone's sweet spot. Phoenix location. No GC assigned yet. Tom should call his network for an intro to Empire Group's project lead immediately.

fewShotExamples.title: DPR Construction wins $80M data center — Chandler
fewShotExamples.reasoning: DPR (competitor) winning a large project = concrete scope opportunity. $80M project means significant structural concrete. Action: call DPR to bid the concrete package. Score 7 not 9 because GC is already assigned.

fewShotExamples.title: Greystar 350-unit multifamily — Tempe Town Lake
fewShotExamples.reasoning: Greystar is a target developer. 350-unit multifamily is perfect Bristlecone sweet spot. Tempe is Phoenix metro. Pre-planning stage means Tom can pitch early preconstruction collaboration. Score 9 — act this week.

fewShotExamples.title: City of Scottsdale approves zoning for senior living facility — N Scottsdale
fewShotExamples.reasoning: Senior living is a possible fit. Just entitled means early timing (good). But no developer or GC named, no project scale. Action: monitor for developer announcement. Score 6 — watch list, not hot.

fewShotExamples.title: Phoenix metro construction activity report — Q2 2026
fewShotExamples.reasoning: Market intelligence report. No specific project, no actionable contact. Useful context for Tom's pipeline understanding but no immediate action. Score 4 — include in briefing for awareness.

fewShotExamples.title: New warehouse loading dock expansion — Buckeye
fewShotExamples.reasoning: Horizontal infrastructure / light industrial expansion. Not Bristlecone's fit type. No structural concrete scope likely. Buckeye is far west valley. Score 2 — drop.

scoreFloors.condition: Target developer's project in Phoenix metro
scoreFloors.note: Any target developer project is always 8+ regardless of other factors

scoreFloors.condition: Multifamily 100+ units with no GC assigned
scoreFloors.note: Bristlecone's core sweet spot with open GC opportunity

scoreFloors.condition: Data center or semiconductor facility in AZ
scoreFloors.note: Large-scale structural concrete scope even if not GC opportunity

scoreFloors.condition: RFP with bid deadline in next 30 days

scoreFloors.note: Concrete scope sub opportunity — call the winning GC
